title: Break second pass into smaller incremental build steps

requirements.text: Implement store management backend support: add createStore, updateStore, getStoresByVendor, and toggleStoreActive query/update methods to the main Motoko actor, persisting store records in stable state.

quotes: break the second pass into smaller steps to make it easier to get through

acceptanceCriteria: createStore creates a new store record associated with the calling vendor principal
updateStore modifies an existing store's name, description, and active status
getStoresByVendor returns all stores for a given vendor ID
toggleStoreActive flips the active boolean on a store record
A vendor cannot exceed 5 stores (enforced in backend)

requirements.text: Wire up store management React Query hooks in useQueries.ts: add useCreateStore, useUpdateStore, useGetStoresByVendor, and useToggleStoreActive hooks that call the corresponding backend methods.

quotes: break the second pass into smaller steps to make it easier to get through

acceptanceCriteria: useGetStoresByVendor fetches stores for the current vendor and caches the result
useCreateStore mutation calls the backend and invalidates the stores query on success
useUpdateStore mutation calls the backend and invalidates the stores query on success
useToggleStoreActive mutation calls the backend and invalidates the stores query on success

requirements.text: Integrate the StoreFormModal component into the vendor UI: connect it to useCreateStore and useUpdateStore hooks so vendors can create and edit stores via the modal form.

quotes: break the second pass into smaller steps to make it easier to get through

acceptanceCriteria: Opening StoreFormModal with no existing store data submits a create request
Opening StoreFormModal with existing store data pre-populates the form and submits an update request
A success toast is shown after successful creation or update
An error toast is shown if the operation fails

requirements.text: Integrate the StoreListManager component into the VendorDashboard page: render the list of the vendor's stores with create, edit, and toggle-active controls fully functional using the store hooks.

quotes: break the second pass into smaller steps to make it easier to get through

acceptanceCriteria: VendorDashboard renders StoreListManager showing all stores for the logged-in vendor
Each store row has an edit button that opens StoreFormModal pre-populated
Each store row has a toggle button that activates or deactivates the store
A 'Create Store' button opens StoreFormModal in creation mode
The list is disabled or shows a message when the vendor already has 5 stores

requirements.text: Integrate the StoreSelector component into the VendorDashboard page so vendors can select their active store context, and persist the selected store ID in component state for use by other vendor panels.

quotes: break the second pass into smaller steps to make it easier to get through

acceptanceCriteria: StoreSelector is rendered in VendorDashboard and populates from the vendor's store list
Selecting a store updates the active store context in the dashboard
The selected store's name and active/inactive status badge are visible in the selector
Product management and other vendor panels receive the selected store ID as context where applicable

constraints: All Motoko logic must remain in the single main actor (backend/main.mo)
Do not modify files under frontend/src/hooks/useInternetIdentity.ts, frontend/src/hooks/useActor.ts, frontend/src/main.tsx, or frontend/src/components/ui
Generate migration.mo only if stable backend state shape changes require it
nonGoals: Redesigning existing UI components unrelated to store management
Adding new product or order features beyond what is needed for store context
Implementing any payment or auction changes in this pass
